title: View all items on a board, without seeing Groups?
excerpt:
Hi Monday Community, 
I’m a Production Manager for a digital media company who has recently adopted Monday, and am seeking a better way to sort. We have 1 board with all of our series on it, and each episode or piece of …

posts.author: NathanielPeterman (Nathaniel Peterman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday Community,

I’m a Production Manager for a digital media company who has recently adopted Monday, and am seeking a better way to sort. We have 1 board with all of our series on it, and each episode or piece of content for that series is a unique item within that series Group. This works for most of our team, as many members are dedicated to a specific series.

As the PM, I am looking to better pull and report on metrics surrounding the activity of all content we produce, shoot dates, launch dates, etc. For this purpose, I am having a difficult time in pulling these across all series into an easily digestible view, as our list of Series is quite long, and this board contains 30+ groups, and growing.

Is there a way to have a view of a board, that removes the breakdown by Group, for view and sorting of all items? Or based on the above I’ve described, appreciate any tips for the goals I am seeking to achieve, thanks!

Screen Shot 2022-03-18 at 10.57.52 AM1938×1044 172 KB

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @NathanielPeterman - monday just released Dynamic Grouping in Alpha, which would be available from the monday.labs section in your settings menu. This allows you to group by most column types and save as an alternate view.

image827×535 47.3 KB

If you had a common entry in every row (say a hidden numeric column defaulted to 1) it would group by that and show all items combined. You could then sort and save the view.

Please keep in mind, this is in Alpha so apt to change or could be removed.

Thanks
Mark

posts.author: NathanielPeterman (Nathaniel Peterman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you Mark!

This is exactly the solution I needed and more.

posts.author: Wes (Wes)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

How is this done now?

This feature is no longer in labs.

Thanks

posts.author: johngcolejr (John Cole, Jr)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updated on this?

posts.author: DavidR (David Rothenberg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there, any updates or alternative for this please?
Thanks

posts.author: Rodrigo (Rodrigo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, any update on this?
Thanks!

posts.author: njw (Nick Williams)
posts.cookedHtml:
I could really use this alternate-view/group functionality for similar reasons to OP. Any way of doing this presently whilst Dynamic Groups isn’t in Monday.Labs?

Thanks

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Currently the Group By Functionality is only available in the Monday DEV product (for whatever reason). Hopefully this will be released to the general public but I haven’t seen this on the roadmap.
Mark

posts.author: MikeNew (Mike New)
posts.cookedHtml: I really need this as well

posts.author: jackieb (Jackie B)
posts.cookedHtml: Same. This feature would solve a lot of issues i’m having.

posts.author: RSHartley (Richard Hartley)
posts.cookedHtml: I need this feature too. Shame it doesn’t seem to currently be possible.

posts.author: jcfretts (Jeremy Fretts)
posts.cookedHtml: Also need this, so that we can get a global view sorted by one of the other columns. In our use case, I have numerous projects (Label; could be Status) on a shared Sprint board, with Groups being used for another purpose.

posts.author: Wombat (Dave Hampson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agree, Groups are my single biggest frustration in Monday.
Especially when using filters you often end up with 1 row per group which takes up loads of space - Not good when you’re trying to fit them into a dashboard.

posts.author: Arno_M (Arnaud)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 ! Hope it will be out soon.

posts.author: apegg (April Pegg)
posts.cookedHtml: This thread was heartbreaking! I was so excited this was an option until I scrolled down. +1 I hope this gets added soon!

posts.author: WeeMeng (Wee Meng Yeo)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this feature to be release in Work OS as well.

posts.author: Luke-ACIPENERGY (Luke Brady)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature would be so helpful to be able to sort projects better, I don’t want to take out groups and make it a status on a column. I would love it to be interchangeable.

posts.author: JoeH (Joe Hardin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Echoing what Dave said, groups are the worst designed part of Monday.

The fact that you are forced to use one in the first place is a problem…
and then you are forced to see groups on any dashboard table widgets …
and at the same time, you cant make groups get created based on columns

The concept of groups is great, but because the design is poor they are, in many cases, more of an impediment than they are a help. Dynamic grouping (and not forcing groups to show on dashboards) would solve a lot of the problems.

posts.author: User129438 (John S)
posts.cookedHtml: Came hear searching for this so I could highlight all of the entries and delete them without having to go through each group individually

posts.author: DipaliPatankar (Dipali Patankar)
posts.cookedHtml:
I wanted the same for a list of events I was building. Although it was easier to create groups for each part of the organisation to add their activities, there was a need to present a consolidated view sorted by date.

My workaround was to create another board with just 1 group. Then add an automation to the first board to create an item in the second board on a trigger action- I used a status change as the trigger but you can choose anything that works .
Add mirror columns from the first board to the second board and voila you have a list sorted by date. I also added a column to the second board to capture the group the item was on the first board.

On the gantt view, you need to explicitely select the mirrored timeline column else it looks blank.

Hope this solution works for you

posts.author: inspiredearth (Jonathan)
posts.cookedHtml: 2+ years later, and I have to agree with you that groups is a badly thought out feature in Monday. They end up being a pain in the arse to deal with.
